Avoid being judgmental or dismissive of others' opinions. 3. Provide evidence-based information to support your arguments. 4. Be willing to compromise and find common ground. 5. Use 'I' statements instead of 'you' statements, which can come across as accusatory. 6. Avoid using jargon or technical terms that may be unfamiliar to the other person. 7. Listen actively and try to understand the other person's perspective. 2. Avoid being judgmental or dismissive of others' opinions. 3. Provide evidence-based information to support your arguments. 4. Be willing to compromise and find common ground. 5. Use 'I' statements instead of 'you' statements, which can come across as accusatory. 6. Avoid using jargon or technical terms that may be unfamiliar to the other person. 7. Encourage the other person to share their thoughts and feelings. 8.
